free_tier_warning: Any plan with 100K+ context tokens works. Free tiers usually don't have enough context — you'll get worse output and sessions that cut off early. A paid plan is the only real requirement.

seven_days.title: Install and your first six files
seven_days.detail: Run the setup wizard. Answer the prompts. The system generates your identity file, priorities, cadence, brain layer. Don't add anything extra. This step alone unlocks daily ops, meeting prep, decision logging, and knowledge capture.

seven_days.title: Your voice and your brand
seven_days.detail: Run the voice interview. Run the brand interview. From this point, every writing skill produces output that sounds like you - not like AI wrote it. This is the step most people skip and then wonder why their emails feel off.

seven_days.title: Log one real thing
seven_days.detail: Something actually happened today. A decision. A conversation that changed something. A deal that moved. Log it. Three lines in brain/log.md. That's the muscle you're building. The first log entry is the hardest. After that it's a habit.

seven_days.title: Run your first rant
seven_days.detail: Day 4 is when you first dump an unstructured thought into the system. No editing. No structure. Use /rant. Just talk. This is how you start capturing what's actually in your head, not just the cleaned-up version.

seven_days.title: Use one skill on real work
seven_days.detail: Not a test. Something you actually need. A meeting prep. An email draft. A LinkedIn post. Let the skill read your brain and produce output. See what comes back. Adjust one thing. This is how the system starts feeling useful, not just set up.

seven_days.title: Process the rant
seven_days.detail: Run /dream on what you captured on Day 4. The skill turns your raw thinking into flags, patterns, and parked decisions. This is how the brain learns what you care about. The rant was raw material. Dream gives it shape.

seven_days.title: Run the weekly retro
seven_days.detail: Look at what you said the week would be. What actually happened. What moves forward. What gets killed. What decision you've been avoiding. This is the heartbeat. Once it's running, it runs. Put it in your calendar. Sunday at 9pm or Monday at 8am.

tune_skills_first_table.why_different: Reads your voice profile so emails sound like you, not like AI. Runs an anti-AI filter against your captured anti-patterns before returning a draft, so generic phrasing gets caught before send. Leans on your brain snapshot so the draft matches what you're actually working on this week.

Reads your stack file so 'CRM' becomes the tool you actually use, not a placeholder. Inherits your voice rhythm so steps don't read like a corporate template. Owner and tools fields stay populated so a delegated reader can act without coming back to ask.

Inherits the colours, fonts, and logo you set during the brand interview. Applies your voice profile so the prose doesn't read like AI. Reads the existing client context file so you don't re-explain who they are or what was last agreed.

Treats the first two lines as a unit because that's the 'see more' cutoff on mobile. Anti-AI filter applies to every draft. content-repurposer takes one piece and adapts it across LinkedIn, email, and other formats so you don't write five things.

meeting-prep pulls client history and prior commitments before the call. /capture-meeting routes the post-call dump into log.md, clients.md, and open commitments in one step. knowledge-capture writes takeaways with stable IDs so you can cite them later.

decision-framework adapts to whether you decide by gut, data, or dialogue and runs the right template. forcing-questions runs six fixed questions that test done state, smallest ship, replacement cost, urgency, and proof. brain-pass synthesises across your log, decisions, flags, and patterns so the answer cites your own evidence.

today gives a 20-line one-screen view: anchor task, open flags, last three log entries, next event. priority-triage cuts to ONE thing, not a list. weekly-review keeps the cadence honest so flags don't pile up.
